source.principle: Bootstrap's release pipeline emits a /*! ... v X.Y.Z ... */ banner at the top of every minified CSS and JS artifact. The /*! prefix is the standard 'preserve through minification' marker honored by uglify/terser/cssnano. Banner has been stable across 3.x, 4.x, and 5.x majors. The 'Bootstrap' token plus optional 'v' and dotted version is the entire signal -- this rule does NOT pin separator or surrounding chars (per CLAUDE.md sec 7 'think signal, not samples'). 5.3.x CSS uses an extra space ('Bootstrap  v5.3.3') -- absorbed by \\s+. Verified against official jsdelivr mirror + 3 self-hosted corpus targets.
rejects_by_design: 'BootstrapCDN' (token would need to be 'Bootstrap ' boundary -- handled by \\s+ requirement)

format_observed: {key:"VERSION",get:function(){return"4.0.0-beta.2"}}  // same source after Babel ES6 class -> ES5 _createClass transpilation
key: "VERSION", get: function get() { return VERSION$a; }  // bootstrap@4.6.2 modular form -- references shared const, this rule does NOT fire here (banner handles 4.6.2)
source.principle: Bootstrap 4.x non-minified bootstrap.js emits 'var VERSION = ...' per component (Util, Alert, Button, etc.) at the producer site -- 11 components total in 4.0.x, each with the literal version string. When a downstream build (Babel ES5 class transpile) reshapes the component into a _createClass call, the literal becomes {key:"VERSION",get:function(){return"X.Y.Z"}}. Both forms share \\bVERSION\\b -> short gap -> quoted dotted-3 version. \\bVERSION\\b enforces word boundary (rejects MY_VERSION, VERSION_TEMPLATE, VERSIONS). 40-char gap absorbs the longest seen transpiled form (24 chars: '",get:function(){return"'). Requires 3 dotted parts to reject incomplete '1.5' style and reduce false positives on unrelated VERSION='1.0' tokens. Source: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@4.0.0-beta.2/dist/js/bootstrap.js (WebFetch 2026-05-18 -- confirmed 11 'var VERSION = ' occurrences, all '4.0.0-beta.2'). Cross-check: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@4.6.2/dist/js/bootstrap.js uses VERSION$N shared-const form which this rule does NOT match (intentional -- banner rule handles 4.6.2).
rejects_by_design: VERSION$a / VERSION$9 in modular 4.6.x+ builds (no quoted literal in same proximity -- banner_bootstrap catches these)
MY_VERSION = '1.2.3' (\\b word-boundary fails because _ is word-char)
VERSIONS = ['1.0.0'] (\\b after N fails -- S is word-char)
VERSION = '1.5' (only 2 dotted parts -- bootstrap always emits 3)
Unrelated VERSION token > 40 chars from the version literal
source.negative_overlap_check: On minified bootstrap.min.js bodies (5sfashion.vn, caresoft) the VERSION literals are mangled away by Terser -- this rule yields None there, banner_bootstrap fires. No double-counting risk. On un-minified or re-transpiled bodies (cofer.edu.vn, chungauto.vn) this rule recovers what the banner_rule misses or duplicates with consistent version.
